subjective.hpi: Patient presents with burning chest discomfort that started approximately 3 weeks ago. Sensation is located in the upper chest/epigastric area, described as burning. Symptoms worse when lying down and after large meals. Improved with over-the-counter antacids. Denies radiation to arm or jaw. No associated diaphoresis or shortness of breath.

objective.physicalExam: Alert and oriented. Mild epigastric tenderness on palpation. No rebound tenderness. Lungs clear to auscultation. Heart regular rate and rhythm.
diagnostics: EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no ST changes

assessment.notes: Most likely cause of chest discomfort given symptom pattern and response to antacids
Ongoing management, fasting lipid panel ordered to assess response to statin therapy
Blood pressure slightly elevated at 142/88; monitoring, dietary counseling provided

items: Start omeprazole (Prilosec) 20mg once daily before breakfast
Avoid GERD triggers: caffeine, spicy foods, large meals before bed
Reduce sodium intake for blood pressure management
30 minutes of moderate exercise most days of the week
Return in 4 weeks to review fasting lipid panel results
If chest discomfort worsens or new symptoms develop, come in sooner
Blood pressure recheck at next visit
